description: Creator explicitly states Abomasnow 'pairs very well with Shadow Quags'. Abomasnow's Ice/Grass typing covers Quagsire's Grass weakness while Quagsire covers Abomasnow's Fire weakness with Water/Ground typing and provides strong spam with Mud Shot.

sources.author: ItsAXN
sources.videoTitle: Abomasnow COMPLETELY Breaks Teams In GBL [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Explicitly called a 'core' by the creator. Shadow Charizard applies massive offensive pressure with Wing Attack/Blast Burn while Aegislash provides exceptional bulk in shield form and hard-hitting Shadow Ball/Gyro Ball pressure. Aegislash walls off threats like Kommo-o that trouble Charizard. Stunfisk Galarian is noted as the main core-breaker.

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: STOP. DISRESPECTING. CHARIZARD.

description: Shadow Hydreigon is extremely attack-oriented and commands shields in the lead, which sets up Aegislash perfectly since it thrives once shields are down — its blade-form Shadow Ball and Gyro Ball deal devastating damage and win charge-move priority after transforming.

sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion
sources.videoTitle: Shadow Hydreigon is Officially INSANE!! | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Double ghost backline — Jellicent handles fire/water types that threaten Aegislash while Aegislash walls fighters and fairies that threaten Jellicent. Creator explicitly calls them a 'double ghost comp in the back.'

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: OPPRESSIVE Shadow Hydreigon Applies INSANE Pressure in the Great League!!

description: Altaria + Empoleon form the backbone core that Grumpig is paired with. Empoleon's steel/water typing covers Altaria's ice and rock weaknesses, while Altaria handles the fighting and grass types that threaten Empoleon.

sources.author: ItsAXN

description: Double Fury Cutter core that overwhelms the Psychic-dominant meta — both hit super effective on Psychics and charge extremely fast, with Samurott offering Hydro Cannon pressure and Barbaracle adding Cross Chop (normals) and Stone Edge (flyers) coverage.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: Fury Cutter Users DOMINATE the TOP PSYCHIC POKEMON | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Both resist Tinkaton's Gigaton Hammer, giving the team a fantastic combo answer to one of the top meta threats. Charjabug also covers fires/flyers that threaten Feraligatr.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: CHARJABUG CONTINUES TO IMPRESS | Great League Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Lickilicky and Charjabug complement each other well — Lickilicky's Normal typing handles ghosts/psychics while Charjabug covers flyers and fairies, and their roles can flip depending on matchup with shared energy-management play.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: CHARJABUG is CLUTCH in Great League | Great League Team | Pokemon GO Battle League

description: The creator explicitly calls this the 'Cloyster Mantine core' — two Water-types resisting the buffed water meta. However he notes it struggles together: both lack answers to Steels (Wing Attack, Sidewave, Poison Sting all resisted) and is vulnerable to Stunfisk leads.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Predicted emerging core that is especially tough for Tinkaton — both Pokémon beat Tinkaton, unlike other meta cores where one member is vulnerable. Creator expects to see more of this pairing.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: No Beyond the HYPE Needed: TINKATON with GIGATON HAMMER will SHAKE UP THE META | Community Day

description: Double Volt Switch backline core — Forretress acts as the lure, drawing out fire and ground types that threaten Magnezone, then Magnezone sweeps what remains. Both running Volt Switch provides relentless electric-type fast move pressure and makes opposing double Volt Switch mirrors come down to energy advantage.

sources.videoTitle: I HIT LEGEND AND TOP 10 IN THE WORLD WITH MAGNEZONE!! | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Grumpig + Oinkologne 'tag team backline' that the creator built specifically to cover Dewott's worst matchups (e.g. Galarian Weezing). Grumpig provides energy-rich Shadow Ball/Dynamic Punch pressure while the super-bulky Oinkologne soaks hits and grinds down with boosted Mud Slaps, together dominating the backline.

sources.videoTitle: This New Meta is AMAZING! I went 9-1 with Dewott and Oinkologne | Pokemon GO PvP

description: Galarian Weezing scares off Wigglytuff and other fairies, which opens up Guzzlord to dominate without its primary counter. Creator added Weezing last-minute specifically to enable Guzzlord, and even swapped Guzzlord's Sludge Bomb for Dragon Claw because the fairy matchup was already covered.

description: Double Flying Press core — the two Pokemon that exclusively have access to Flying Press, a busted and highly efficient move, giving the team overwhelming fighting/flying pressure.

description: Jellicent covers Shadow Typhlosion's critical ground weakness. With ground types drawn out and eliminated by Jellicent, Typhlosion roams free in the back with devastating Shadow Blast Burns.

description: Existing meta core — Jellicent beats Tinkaton but its typical partner Wigglytuff gets absolutely walled by Tinkaton, so this core is exploitable by Tinkaton teams.

description: Kangaskhan + Sylveon combo: Kangaskhan's Fake Out locks the opponent down and prevents them from attacking, giving slow Sylveon a free turn to fire off a heavy Hyper Voice/Moonblast for massive chip across the field. Creator calls them staples on his team since day one and easily combos them right off the bat.

description: The two Pokémon Dan found that best break the dominant top-meta of Miltank/Grumpig/Hypno — Galarian Slowking uses Shadow Ball to hit the meta psychics while Shadow Kingdra adds neutral pressure, together posting a strong spread of wins.

description: The most used Great League core from last season — Morpeko's electric pressure pairs with Talonflame's flying/fire coverage. Originally paired with Corviknight, but the Mud Shot buff (and prevalence of Quagsire) hurt that version, so Florges replaces Corviknight here.

description: Sylveon acts as the safe swap that bullies the meta and builds energy, softening and shielding-pressuring opponents to set up Tinkaton, whose Gigaton Hammer cleans up even resisted targets — the creator explicitly built the team around Sylveon setting up Tinkaton.
